name: Workflows Bundle
description: Mirrors skills-bundle for the Workflow tool: bundled orchestration scripts live as strings in @code-workbench/mcp-core (packages/mcp-core/workflows/<name>.cjs exporting {name, script}, indexed by workflows.{cjs,mjs,d.ts,d.cts} as BUNDLED_WORKFLOWS, exposed via the "./workflows" package export). The extension's writeWorkflowScript(ctx, name) in packages/extension/src/workflowsBundle.ts looks the workflow up, mkdir -p's <globalStorage>/workflows/, and unconditionally overwrites <name>.workflow.js with the bundled script, returning its absolute path. Every command invocation rewrites the file, so it is always current with the installed extension version and there is no drift-detection machinery (unlike skills, which are installed into the user's repo and can go stale). Nothing is added to the user's repo — the file lives under VS Code's per-extension globalStorage directory. esbuild inlines each workflow's script string into dist/extension.js via the same require chain that already inlines skills.cjs, so no .vscodeignore or copy-step changes are needed.
guidelines: A bundled script's `export const meta = {...}` must be a pure literal — no variables, function calls, spreads, or template interpolation — since the Workflow tool parses it without executing arbitrary code first.
Scripts are plain JavaScript, not TypeScript: no type annotations, interfaces, or generics.
Never call Date.now(), Math.random(), or argless new Date() in a script — they throw at runtime because they would break workflow resume. Vary agent labels/prompts by array index instead.
Every phase('X') call in the script body needs a matching entry in meta.phases with the same title.
To add a workflow: create packages/mcp-core/workflows/<name>.cjs exporting {name, script} and add it to BUNDLED_WORKFLOWS in workflows.cjs — mirror the skills.cjs index exactly.
writeWorkflowScript always overwrites — do not add change-detection or prompt-before-overwrite logic; the whole point is that it's always current.
anti_patterns: Resolving workflow scripts by name convention under .claude/workflows/ in the user's repo — rejected: it's an unverified convention, adds git noise, and would need its own drift-detection prompt.
Adding drift-detection (stale/missing checks like skillsBundle's checkWorkbenchSkills) for workflow scripts — unnecessary since every run rewrites the file unconditionally.
